ecrScanOnPush.^description: {$COUNT} ECR repositor(y|ies) have no scan-on-push configured (repo-level scanOnPush=false and no registry-level scanning rule matches). Images pushed to these repos may reach production with unknown CVEs; ECR.1 requires scan-on-push. Enabling either the repo-level flag or a registry-level SCAN_ON_PUSH / CONTINUOUS_SCAN rule closes this gap.

ecrTagImmutability.^description: {$COUNT} ECR repositor(y|ies) have imageTagMutability=MUTABLE. Mutable tags allow an existing tag (e.g. `latest`, `v1`) to be overwritten with a different image digest — enabling supply-chain attacks where a compromised push swaps a trusted tag out from under running deployments. ECR.2 requires immutability.

ecrLifecyclePolicy.^description: {$COUNT} ECR repositor(y|ies) have no lifecycle policy configured. Without lifecycle rules, old and untagged image layers accumulate indefinitely — increasing storage cost and expanding the attack surface (older base images are more likely to contain unpatched CVEs). ECR.3 requires a lifecycle policy.
ecrLifecyclePolicy.shortDesc: Attach a lifecycle policy that cleans up untagged / old images

ecrEncryptionKms.^description: {$COUNT} ECR repositor(y|ies) are not encrypted with a customer-managed KMS key (encryptionType=AES256, or KMS with the AWS-managed `aws/ecr` alias). A customer-managed CMK gives you control over key rotation, key policy, and CloudTrail-audited access, and lets you revoke image access by disabling the key. ECR.5 requires KMS encryption.
ecrEncryptionKms.shortDesc: Recreate the repo with encryptionType=KMS and a customer-managed key

ecrCriticalVulnerabilities.^description: {$COUNT} ECR repositor(y|ies) contain image(s) with CRITICAL severity vulnerabilities in the last scan summary. CRITICAL findings are typically remote-exploitable and should be treated as a break-glass event — rebuild the base image with the current patch set or pin to a known-good digest.
ecrCriticalVulnerabilities.shortDesc: Rebuild and push new images to eliminate CRITICAL CVEs

ecrHighVulnerabilities.^description: {$COUNT} ECR repositor(y|ies) contain image(s) with HIGH severity vulnerabilities in the last scan summary. HIGH findings should be scheduled for the next patch cycle; sustained HIGH count is a signal that base-image refresh cadence is too slow.
ecrHighVulnerabilities.shortDesc: Schedule a rebuild against the current patch set

ecrEnhancedScanning.^description: The ECR registry in this region is running BASIC image scanning (Clair-only, scan-on-push snapshot). Enhanced scanning via Amazon Inspector adds continuous scanning of already-pushed images, OS + language-package coverage, and Security Hub integration. Basic scanning misses CVEs disclosed after the initial push.
ecrEnhancedScanning.shortDesc: Enable Enhanced scanning (scanType=ENHANCED) on the registry

ecrScanFrequency.^description: The ECR registry in this region has no rule with scanFrequency=CONTINUOUS_SCAN. Without continuous scanning, images that were CVE-free at push time will not be re-evaluated as new vulnerabilities are disclosed — the finding surfaces only on the next push, which may never happen for pinned production images.
ecrScanFrequency.shortDesc: Add a registry scanning rule with scanFrequency=CONTINUOUS_SCAN

ecrRepoPublicAccess.^description: {$COUNT} ECR repositor(y|ies) have an Allow statement in the repository policy whose Principal is a wildcard (`*` or `{"AWS": "*"}`) without a scoping Condition. Unrestricted wildcard access lets any AWS account (in the worst case, unauthenticated pulls) fetch container images — potentially exposing proprietary code, embedded secrets, or internal build artefacts.
ecrRepoPublicAccess.shortDesc: Remove or scope wildcard-Principal Allow statements

ecrRepoCrossAccount.^description: {$COUNT} ECR repositor(y|ies) grant Allow permissions to external AWS accounts in the repository policy without an aws:PrincipalOrgID Condition. Broad cross-account access widens blast radius: if any partner account is compromised, its identities can pull (and in some cases push) images. Restrict with aws:PrincipalOrgID or aws:SourceAccount.

ecrUntaggedImages.^description: {$COUNT} ECR repositor(y|ies) contain untagged images — usually orphaned layers left behind when a tag was moved to a newer image. Untagged images continue to accrue storage cost, cannot be referenced by pull commands, and are typically safe to remove via a lifecycle rule targeting `tagStatus=untagged`.
ecrUntaggedImages.shortDesc: Add a lifecycle rule targeting tagStatus=untagged

ecrStaleImages.^description: {$COUNT} ECR repositor(y|ies) contain image(s) that have not been pulled in 90+ days (or have no recorded pull time). Stale images occupy storage, may carry unpatched CVEs, and indicate deployments that no longer reference them. Consider a lifecycle rule that expires images past a pull-age threshold.
ecrStaleImages.shortDesc: Prune images that have not been pulled recently

ecrImageNeverScanned.^description: {$COUNT} ECR repositor(y|ies) contain image(s) with no usable scan status (imageScanStatus is None, FAILED, UNSUPPORTED_IMAGE, or SCAN_ELIGIBILITY_EXPIRED). Images without scan results are a security blind spot — CVE posture is unknown. Re-trigger scans manually or push a fresh image tag to force a scan.

ecrReplicationNotConfigured.^description: The ECR registry in this region has no replication rules configured. In a regional outage or account-isolation scenario, container-based deployments in other regions cannot pull images. Cross-region replication produces a second copy of every push in the destination region(s).
ecrReplicationNotConfigured.shortDesc: Configure cross-region ECR replication for critical repositories

ecrPullThroughCache.^description: The ECR registry in this region has no pull-through cache rules. Without pull-through cache, deployments that reference upstream images (Docker Hub, Quay, public.ecr.aws, GitHub Container Registry, K8s registry) hit the external registry directly on every cache miss — subject to that registry's rate limits and availability. Pull-through cache in ECR gives you local mirroring, IAM-scoped pulls, and image-scanning coverage of the cached copies.
ecrPullThroughCache.shortDesc: Configure pull-through cache rules for external registries

ecrImageAge.^description: {$COUNT} ECR repositor(y|ies) contain image(s) pushed more than 365 days ago that have never been pulled (or have not been pulled in 90+ days). Very old, un-pulled images are strong candidates for cleanup — they contribute to storage cost and typically only exist because a lifecycle rule is missing.
ecrImageAge.shortDesc: Add a lifecycle rule with countType=sinceImagePushed

ecrPublicRepoTagging.^description: {$COUNT} ECR repositor(y|ies) have no tags applied. Tags drive cost allocation, ownership, environment separation, and access control (via aws:ResourceTag policy conditions). Absence of tags makes governance and chargeback impractical. ECR.4 requires public repositories to carry identifying tags.
ecrPublicRepoTagging.shortDesc: Apply owner / environment / cost-center tags to every repository

ecrKmsDsseEncryption.^description: {$COUNT} ECR repositor(y|ies) are not using dual-layer server-side encryption (encryptionType != KMS_DSSE). KMS_DSSE applies two independent layers of AES-256 encryption to each object with two distinct KMS data keys; the feature is intended for workloads that need to satisfy specific regulatory profiles (FedRAMP High, DoD IL-5+). Not required for most environments — advisory only.

ecrLifecyclePolicyEffectiveness.^description: {$COUNT} ECR repositor(y|ies) have a lifecycle policy configured but the ruleset is missing at least one of: (a) an `tagStatus=untagged` cleanup rule, or (b) a `countType=sinceImagePushed` age-based cleanup rule. Without both, either orphaned layers or aged tagged images will still accumulate.
ecrLifecyclePolicyEffectiveness.shortDesc: Add both untagged and age-based rules to the lifecycle policy

ecrScanFindingsStale.^description: {$COUNT} ECR repositor(y|ies) have image(s) whose most-recent scan (imageScanCompletedAt) is older than 30 days. Stale scan data hides CVEs disclosed since the last scan. Enabling CONTINUOUS_SCAN (see ecrScanFrequency) refreshes findings automatically; otherwise re-scan periodically.
ecrScanFindingsStale.shortDesc: Re-scan images with stale findings (or enable CONTINUOUS_SCAN)
